data.title: Metsä Board (HLSE:METSB) Widening Losses Test Bullish Turnaround Narratives Heading Into Q1 2026
data.description:
Metsä Board Oyj (HLSE:METSB) has opened Q1 2026 under the shadow of recent losses, with Q4 2025 revenue at €402.6 million and a basic EPS loss of €0.25 alongside net income excluding extra items of a €89.9 million loss. Over recent quarters, revenue has moved from €480.8 million in Q1 2025 to €460.1 million in Q2, €441.2 million in Q3 and €402.6 million in Q4, while EPS shifted from a €0.02 loss in Q1 2025 to a €0.06 loss in Q2, a €0.11 loss in Q3 and a €0.25 loss in Q4. Together, these figures set a clear earnings backdrop for the new quarter. For investors, the focus now is on whether margins can start to stabilize following this run of quarterly losses.

See our full analysis for Metsä Board Oyj.

Losses Widen To €156.6 Million Over The Last Year

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, Metsä Board reported net income excluding extra items of a €156.6 million loss, compared with a small profit of €25.3 million just over a year earlier when trailing net income was positive.
  • Consensus narrative points to a planned EBITDA improvement program targeting a €200 million uplift by 2027. However, the recent shift from a €25.3 million profit in Q4 2024 to a €89.9 million loss in Q4 2025 means investors are weighing that planned improvement against a period where losses have grown at about 51% per year.
    • Analysts expect earnings to move from a €156.6 million loss today to €86.8 million of earnings by around 2029, which would be a clear swing if delivered, but current trailing figures still show an unprofitable position.
    • With revenue on the trailing 12 month view at €1.78b versus €1.94b previously, the consensus view that revenue can grow about 2.9% per year has to be considered alongside this recent revenue softening.

Top Line Softens As Forecasts Point To 7.2% Growth

  • Trailing 12 month revenue has stepped down from €1.94b when Q4 2024 was included to €1.78b by Q4 2025, even though revenue is forecast to grow at about 7.2% per year, which is faster than the Finnish market forecast of 4.2% per year.
  • Bullish investors argue that cost savings and capacity upgrades can support that faster growth. Yet the recent pattern from €499 million in Q3 2024 to €402.6 million in Q4 2025 shows the business coming off a higher base while still unprofitable.
    • The optimistic view leans on projected earnings growth of about 25.95% per year and a move back to profitability within three years, while the latest four quarters together still show a €156.6 million loss.
    • Bulls also point to the reported DCF fair value of €3.49 versus the current share price of €2.85, implying potential upside if those revenue and margin forecasts are met, but the recent revenue trend leaves little room for execution missteps.
Bulls argue that recent capacity investments and cost programs could turn this €156.6 million loss into faster profit growth than consensus expects, which is exactly the scenario explored in the full bull case narrative for Metsä Board Oyj.🐂 Metsä Board Oyj Bull Case

Valuation Gap Meets Bearish Concerns On Profitability

  • At a share price of €2.85 and trailing 12 month revenue of €1.78b, Metsä Board trades on a P/S of about 0.6x, below the 2.1x peer average and roughly aligned with the European Packaging industry average of 0.6x, while analysts reference a DCF fair value of €3.49 and a consensus price target of €2.88.
  • Bears highlight that the company is still loss making with a €156.6 million trailing loss and that losses have grown at about 51% per year, which they see as a concern even if the stock currently sits below both the DCF fair value and the €2.88 analyst target.
    • The cautious narrative points out that, despite forecasts for earnings to reach €128.1 million or even €86.8 million by the late 2020s, the last four reported quarters each showed losses, including a €89.9 million loss in Q4 2025 alone.
    • Critics also note that while revenue is expected to grow between roughly 2.9% and 7.2% per year depending on the scenario, the recent move from €499 million in Q3 2024 to €402.6 million in Q4 2025 means the business is currently operating below that future revenue ambition.

data.title: CONMED (CNMD) Q1 Margin Compression To 4% Tests Bullish Earnings Growth Narrative
data.description:
CONMED (CNMD) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of US$317.0 million and basic EPS of US$0.45, setting the tone for how margins are holding up after a year of shifting profitability. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from US$321.3 million and EPS of US$0.19 in Q1 2025 to US$317.0 million and EPS of US$0.45 in Q1 2026, while trailing 12 month revenue sits at about US$1.4 billion and EPS at US$1.77. This puts the focus squarely on how efficiently that top line is being converted into profit.

See our full analysis for CONMED.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the big narratives around growth, margins and profit quality that investors have been watching.

See what the community is saying about CONMED

Advertisement

Margins Under Pressure, Despite US$1.4b Trailing Sales

  • Over the last 12 months, CONMED generated about US$1.4b in revenue with net income of US$54.8 million, which works out to a 4% net margin compared with 9% a year earlier.
  • Consensus narrative expects operational improvements and higher-margin products to support better profitability over time. The current 4% margin and the earlier 9% level highlight the gap that needs to be closed.
    • Analysts are cited as looking for earnings growth of around 30.8% per year over the next three years, compared with revenue growth of about 3.8% per year. This implies a focus on margins doing more of the heavy lifting than sales growth.
    • The trailing margin step down, combined with this forecasted earnings ramp, gives you a clear point to compare future reports against, especially if costs or pricing move differently from what the narrative assumes.

TTM Earnings Growth vs 4% Margin Squeeze

  • Over the past five years, earnings grew at about 25.7% per year, yet the latest trailing 12 month net profit margin sits at 4%, down from 9% in the prior 12 month period.
  • Bulls argue that supply chain fixes and cost savings can support stronger profitability, and the historical 25.7% earnings growth rate is often used as a reference point. However, the recent 4% margin keeps the bullish case heavily dependent on future execution.
    • The bullish narrative points to at least US$20 million in annualized savings from operational efficiencies and expects margins to rise over time, which contrasts with the current drop from 9% to 4% in the trailing numbers.
    • For a beginner investor, this means the bullish story leans on management turning cost actions and product mix into a margin profile that looks closer to the historical trend than to the latest 12 month snapshot.
Sustained margin improvement is central to what optimistic investors are betting on, so if you want to see how that full upside case is laid out, check out the 🐂 CONMED Bull Case.

Low Margin, High Debt Keep Bears Engaged

  • The trailing 12 month period includes a US$26.0 million one off loss and a net margin of 4%, and the company is described as having a high level of debt on the balance sheet.
  • Bears highlight that slower forecast revenue growth of about 3.8% per year, a compressed 4% margin, the US$26.0 million one off loss, and elevated leverage together leave less room for error if costs stay high or procedure volumes soften.
    • The cautious narrative leans on rising healthcare cost controls, tariff and supply chain risks, and pressure on hospital budgets, which would all make it harder to improve on a 4% margin starting point.
    • When reading future filings, you can compare any movement in margins or debt levels against this baseline to see whether the more cautious story or the optimistic one is getting more support from the numbers.
Skeptical investors are watching whether these balance sheet and margin issues ease or persist, and you can see that full cautious case in the 🐻 CONMED Bear Case.

data.title: Here's What Analysts Are Forecasting For TDK Corporation (TSE:6762) After Its Annual Results
data.description:
Investors in TDK Corporation (TSE:6762) had a good week, as its shares rose 8.6% to close at JP¥2,891 following the release of its yearly results. Results were roughly in line with estimates, with revenues of JP¥2.5t and statutory earnings per share of JP¥103. Earnings are an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance, look at what the analysts are forecasting for next year, and see if there's been a change in sentiment towards the company. So we collected the latest post-earnings statutory consensus estimates to see what could be in store for next year.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Taking into account the latest results, the current consensus from TDK's 16 analysts is for revenues of JP¥2.59t in 2027. This would reflect a credible 3.2% increase on its revenue over the past 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are predicted to rise 9.9% to JP¥113. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of JP¥2.60t and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥116 in 2027. The analysts seem to have become a little more negative on the business after the latest results, given the minor downgrade to their earnings per share numbers for next year.

Check out our latest analysis for TDK

The consensus price target held steady at JP¥2,761, with the analysts seemingly voting that their lower forecast earnings are not expected to lead to a lower stock price in the foreseeable future. There's another way to think about price targets though, and that's to look at the range of price targets put forward by analysts, because a wide range of estimates could suggest a diverse view on possible outcomes for the business. The most optimistic TDK analyst has a price target of JP¥3,500 per share, while the most pessimistic values it at JP¥1,600. Note the wide gap in analyst price targets? This implies to us that there is a fairly broad range of possible scenarios for the underlying business.

One way to get more context on these forecasts is to look at how they compare to both past performance, and how other companies in the same industry are performing. It's pretty clear that there is an expectation that TDK's revenue growth will slow down substantially, with revenues to the end of 2027 expected to display 3.2% growth on an annualised basis. This is compared to a historical growth rate of 6.9% over the past five years. Compare this against other companies (with analyst forecasts) in the industry, which are in aggregate expected to see revenue growth of 7.7% annually. Factoring in the forecast slowdown in growth, it seems obvious that TDK is also expected to grow slower than other industry participants.

The Bottom Line

The biggest concern is that the analysts reduced their earnings per share estimates, suggesting business headwinds could lay ahead for TDK. On the plus side, there were no major changes to revenue estimates; although forecasts imply they will perform worse than the wider industry. The consensus price target held steady at JP¥2,761, with the latest estimates not enough to have an impact on their price targets.
